channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges Tuesday against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, KREX 5

item.title: Naval Academy removes hundreds of library books in DEI purge
item.description: Officials for the Annapolis, Md., school were instructed to review the library late last week, though it is unclear if the directive came straight from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Turkish student detained by ICE moved to Vermont before judge's order, government says
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities had been moved to Vermont by the time a federal judge ordered authorities to keep her in Massachusetts, lawyers for the U.S. government said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin and Florida elections provide early warning signs to Trump and Republicans
item.description: A trio of spring elections offered early warning signs to Republicans and President Donald Trump, as Democrats rallied against his efforts to slash the federal government and the outsize role being played by billionaire Elon Musk in the early days of his new administration.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MAYA SWEEDLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Final report details investigation into deadly 2023 Covenant School shooting
item.description: The report touches on the shooter's planning as well as the details unveiled during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at Trump's DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk and President Donald Trump have recently suggested that the billionaire entrepreneur's time in the administration might be running out.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump is set to announce new tariffs as he takes a political and financial gamble
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After weeks of White House hype and public anxiety, President Donald Trump is set on Wednesday to announce a barrage of what he calls reciprocal tariffs on friend and foe alike. The new tariffs, coming on what Trump has named “Liberation Day,” are a bid to boost U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Plastic bag found in dead bottlenose dolphin's stomach
item.description: Tuesday, Mar. 25, 2025, a large, dead bottlenose dolphin was reported on the banks of Deep Creek in the Rachel Carson Estuarine Reserve in Beaufort.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Beachy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Plane bounces on Tampa airport runway while trying to land, video shows
item.description: A dramatic video captured a small plane bouncing on the runway of Tampa International Airport while attempting a landing, forcing it to go around a second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Average mortgage payments hit new record high
item.description: A typical monthly housing payment hit a record high of $2,807 during the last four weeks ending March 23, according to a study by Redfin. The 5.3% increase is a result of the average cost of home prices going up roughly 3% annually and mortgage rates sitting at 6.6%.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, KTLA 5

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dirt bikes, ATVs driven around World War II memorial in Washington, DC: video
item.description: Authorities are working to identify people seen on video driving ATVs and dirt bikes inside the World War II memorial in Washington, D.C., over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actor Val Kilmer dies at 65
item.description: Val Kilmer, a veteran actor known for his roles as Jim Morrison and Batman, has died on Tuesday, his family confirmed to the New York Times. He was 65. His daughter, Mercedes Kilmer, said her father died of pneumonia and was surrounded by family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: Republican Jimmy Patronis wins special election for Gaetz's Florida seat
item.description: Florida chief financial officer Jimmy Patronis was projected to win the special election to replace former Rep. Matt Gatez (R-Fla.) in the 1st Congressional District, defeating Democrat Gay Valimont, according to Decision Desk HQ.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amish buggies targeted in Pennsylvania robbery, attempted robbery
item.description: Some among Pennsylvania's Amish community are concerned that someone may be targeting them, after two buggies were held up at gunpoint, according to State Police.
item.dc:creator: Stan Boney, KRON4

item.title: Booker sets record for longest Senate speech in history
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Tuesday set a new record for longest floor speech in Senate history, having held the floor for 25 hours and 5 minutes to decry potential GOP spending cuts in their looming tax bill and policies put in place by the Trump administration. Booker, the No.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Dog of captain who lost boats in Lahaina wildfire barks with delight during whale encounter
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A Hawaii boat captain who rebuilt her whale-watching tour business after losing three boats in the deadly 2023 Lahaina wildfire captured iPhone footage of her dog barking excitedly when a humpback swam near them over the weekend and poked its head out to greet Macy, a golden retrieve
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rejects Trump administration’s bid to move Mahmoud Khalil’s legal case to Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that the legal battle over Mahmoud Khalil’s deportation should continue to play out in New Jersey, rejecting the Trump administration’s bid to transfer the Columbia University protester’s case to Louisiana. In a written decision Tuesday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Astronaut reunites with dogs after 9 months 'stuck' in space
item.description: Touching video shows NASA astronaut Suni Williams reuniting with her dogs after a longer-than-expected stay on board the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Northern Michigan
item.description: The tip of Michigan's mitten struggled through another day without electricity Tuesday as restless residents tried to stay warm while utilities scrambled to restore power in a region waylaid by weekend freezing rain that brought down countless trees and poles.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Attorney General instructs prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi instructed prosecutors to seek the death penalty for Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthCare's CEO.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Federal prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
item.description: It is the first time the Justice Department has sought to bring the death penalty since President Donald Trump returned to office in January.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Miami's Haitian community gathers in prayer as crises escalate in homeland and US
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Packed pews, rollicking singing and emotional devotions have marked Lent worship services at Notre Dame d'Haiti, the Catholic church at the heart of the largest Haitian diaspora in the United States.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump has dubbed April 2 'Liberation Day' for his tariffs. Here's what to expect
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As the trade wars launched by U.S. President Donald Trump continue to escalate, all eyes are on Wednesday. Trump has repeatedly called April 2 “Liberation Day,” with promises to roll out a set of tariffs, or taxes on imports from other countries, that he says will free the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia lawmakers agree on school safety bill after Apalachee High School shooting
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia lawmakers said repeatedly that a student-tracking database had been removed from a school safety bill, but parts of that plan survive.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos show living conditions of Connecticut man allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: Dozens of photos obtained by Nexstar's WTNH show the living conditions authorities found in the Waterbury, Connecticut, home where a man says he was held captive by his stepmom, Kimberly Sullivan, for over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Report: 1 in 5 Catholics in US affected by deportations
item.description: One in five Catholic families in the United States stands to feel the pain from the Trump administration's promised mass deportations, a group of faith leaders said.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Federal employees union sues over Trump move to block collective bargaining
item.description: A federal employee union has sued to block a Trump administration order that would strip bargaining rights at more than a dozen departments.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Driver facing multiple charges in Brooklyn crash that killed mother, two children
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A woman is facing multiple charges after she crashed into a family out for a walk in Brooklyn, killing a mother and her two children in what New York City Mayor Eric Adams described as a “tragic accident of a Shakespearean proportion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks release of some records related to deaths of Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa
item.description: A New Mexico judge has decided to block the release of public records showing the bodies of Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Cruz, Fox 5

item.title: Apparent explosion, fire injures about a dozen in Detroit apartment building
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — About a dozen people have been injured, including six children, following an apparent explosion and fire Monday morning at a 2-story apartment building in Detroit. The explosion was reported about 4 a.m., according to fire officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Freezing rain brought down trees and power lines in Michigan and Wisconsin, cutting electricity for thousands of people Sunday in the upper Great Lakes region, while forecasters said severe weather was on its way to Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suns star Kevin Durant helped off court after injuring left leg
item.description: Phoenix Suns All-Star Kevin Durant was helped to the locker room with 6:57 left in the third quarter against Houston on Sunday night after falling to the floor in a collision with the Rockets' Jabari Smith Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: The president threatened to slap a new tariff on Russia if it is at fault for stalling an end "to bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Utah woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, WGN 9

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need to make a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9
